subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand that science is constantly evolving
Analyze how evidence supports scientific ideas
Apply critical thinking to evaluate hypotheses and theories
Understand the role of experiments and repeatability
Explore collaboration and peer review in scientific progress
Develop problem-solving and analytical skills in real-world contexts

lesson_plan.title: Nature and Evolution of Science
teaching_strategy: Use of case studies showing changing theories
student_activities: Identify examples of scientific ideas that changed over time
Discuss reasons for changes in understanding
Make a timeline of key discoveries

lesson_plan.title: Evidence, Observations, and Hypotheses
teaching_strategy: Guided questioning on observation vs hypothesis

lesson_plan.title: Experiments and Repeatability
topics_covered: Common sources of errors and how to minimize them
teaching_strategy: Discussion on how repeating tests increases reliability
student_activities: Record results and compare with other groups

lesson_plan.title: Critical Thinking and Data Analysis
topics_covered: Distinguishing between opinion and scientific explanation
teaching_strategy: Class discussion on reliability of results

lesson_plan.title: Collaboration, Peer Review, and Scientific Models
topics_covered: Using models to simplify complex systems
student_activities: Work in groups to analyze a problem
Simulate peer review by evaluating another group's experiment
Use a model to explain a scientific phenomenon

lesson_plan.title: Revision, Critical Thinking Quiz, and Real-World Applications
topics_covered: Recap of scientific methods, hypotheses, and theories
teaching_strategy: Quiz combining conceptual and analytical questions
Discussion of real-world applications (technology, healthcare, environment)
student_activities: Relate scientific principles to daily life

teacher_tip: Focus on encouraging reasoning and evaluation skills. Use historical examples to illustrate how science evolves and why evidence and repeatability are essential.
